headline: 17 Temple Demolitions in Selangor Since 2020 Despite Court Orders
context: The Malaysian Hindu Sangam documented 17 Hindu temple demolitions in Selangor between 2020 and 2025, including 4 where court injunctions were in force at the time of demolition.

cards.big: 4 temples were demolished while court injunctions were active.
cards.sub: Local authorities proceeded despite court orders. No contempt proceedings followed.

cards.big: An estimated 3,200 Hindu temples in Malaysia lack proper land title.
cards.sub: Most were built on estate land before independence. When estates were developed, temples had no legal standing.

cards.big: The 2009 Cabinet Committee on Temple Relocation has relocated only 34 temples.
cards.sub: At the current rate, the backlog of 3,200 untitled temples will take 280 years.

cards.big: Hindu Malaysians comprise 6.3% of the population.
cards.sub: Temple demolitions disproportionately affect a minority community with limited political leverage.

cards.big: When court orders do not stop demolitions, minority rights exist on paper but not on the ground.
Temple regularisation requires a dedicated land audit, fast-track titling process, and meaningful penalties for demolishing protected structures. The 2009 committee approach has demonstrably failed.
